payload.description: This is a sandbox game in space setting for virtual reality systems. Initially this game is intended for uni diploma defence.

issue.title: Add Pipeline ID Support and Fix Parsing Table Display
issue.body:
### Problem
1. **Pipeline Visibility Issue**: SourceTracker, ProvenanceTracker, and batch processing components don't support `pipeline_id`, so their progress isn't visible in the consolidated pipeline view showing all modules together.

2. **Parsing Display Issue**: Extraction counts (tables, images, pages) from DoclingParser are not showing in the progress display, making it unclear what was extracted from documents.

### Expected Behavior
- All trackers and batch processors should support `pipeline_id` to appear in unified pipeline view
- Parsing progress should display extraction counts (tables, images, pages) with Docling emphasis
- Users should see exactly what was extracted from documents in the progress tracker

comment.body:
The string interpolation is unnecessary as there are no placeholders in the string. Simply use "elasticvue" instead of $"elasticvue" for clarity and minor performance improvement.
```suggestion
        containerName ??= "elasticvue";
```

mb_rstring is only available from php 8.1

lets not add confusion over this for now.

comment.body:
**<sub><sub>![P1 Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/P1-orange?style=flat)</sub></sub>  Replace hardcoded session/user IDs in checkin payload**

The new check-in payload hardcodes `session_id` and `user_id`, which means every submission from this page will be stored under the same identifiers. When multiple users or concurrent sessions hit this flow, their check-ins will collide, and downstream logic (e.g., `save_suds` in `backend/routers/suds.py`, which selects the latest `emotion_checkins` row by `session_id`) will associate the wrong intake data with a SUDS record or Notion export. This can corrupt user data attribution in any multi-user environment; the IDs need to be derived from the actual session/user state before inserting.

Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
